playbooks.title: Ingestion stuck or failed
symptoms: Ingestion jobs remain in accepted, queued, processing, failed, or partial status longer than the operating band.
New source files or API ingests do not appear in portfolio support overview or load-run progress.
dashboards_metrics: ingestion job status counts by status and age

database_safe_checks.description: Read only the affected job row and recent failure history.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT job_id, status, correlation_id, created_at, updated_at FROM ingestion_jobs WHERE job_id = :job_id;

containment_actions: Pause new bulk submissions for the affected source family.
Use governed retry or bookkeeping repair endpoints only when failure details mark the action as supported.
Do not rerun source files blindly when the response marks retry_safe as false.
escalation_path: Escalate to the ingestion owner when job failure phase is validation, persistence, publish, or bookkeeping.
Escalate to platform operations when readiness points to database, Kafka, or topic bootstrap.
post_incident_evidence: Affected job IDs and correlation IDs

playbooks.title: Consumer DLQ growth
symptoms: DLQ event counts increase for one topic or consumer group.
Kafka consumer telemetry shows terminal failure, dlq, or validation-error outcomes.
dashboards_metrics: consumer DLQ event age and count

database_safe_checks.description: Read only recent DLQ records for the affected topic and group.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT event_id, topic, group_id, correlation_id, replay_status, created_at FROM consumer_dlq_events WHERE topic = :topic ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 25;

containment_actions: Stop replay attempts until the error family is understood.
Preserve redacted payload excerpts and correlation IDs for forensic evidence.
Do not copy raw DLQ payloads into tickets or logs.
escalation_path: Escalate validation-error families to the producing service owner.
Escalate repeated publish or commit failures to platform Kafka operations.

playbooks.title: Replay failure
symptoms: Replay requests return conflict, duplicate-blocked, publish-failed, or audit-write-failed outcomes.
Replay audit records do not show a successful terminal outcome.
dashboards_metrics: consumer DLQ replay audit outcome counts

database_safe_checks.description: Read only replay audit rows for the affected correlation ID.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT audit_id, correlation_id, outcome, recovery_path, created_at FROM consumer_dlq_replay_audit WHERE correlation_id = :correlation_id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 25;

containment_actions: Do not issue repeated replay commands for the same event without checking replay audit.
If audit persistence fails, restore the audit dependency before retrying.
Use only governed replay endpoints; do not republish directly to Kafka.
escalation_path: Escalate publish failures to Kafka/platform operations.
Escalate stale lifecycle conflicts to the ingestion owner.

playbooks.title: Outbox backlog
symptoms: Outbox pending, retry-waiting, or terminal-failed counts grow.
Downstream consumers stop receiving expected source-ingestion or recovery events.

database_safe_checks.description: Read only status counts by topic and event type.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT topic, event_type, status, count(*) FROM outbox_events GROUP BY topic, event_type, status;

containment_actions: Check whether failures are retry-waiting before requeueing.
Use governed requeue endpoints only for terminal failed events with operator reason.
Do not update outbox status directly in the database.
escalation_path: Escalate topic or producer failures to platform Kafka operations.
Escalate payload contract failures to the producing service owner.

playbooks.title: Valuation or aggregation lag
symptoms: Portfolio valuations, position time series, or aggregation state remain stale after ingestion.
Support overview shows queued or processing job counts that are not draining.
dashboards_metrics: valuation job pending and processing counts
aggregation job pending and stale processing counts

database_safe_checks.description: Read only job status counts for the affected portfolio.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT status, count(*) FROM portfolio_valuation_jobs WHERE portfolio_id = :portfolio_id GROUP BY status;

containment_actions: Avoid reseeding or replaying transactions until job lag cause is classified.
Verify scheduler publish and delivery-confirmation posture before resetting stale jobs.
Use support pages to identify whether lag is valuation, aggregation, or upstream source-data related.
escalation_path: Escalate valuation job failures to the valuation owner.
Escalate aggregation queue and scheduler failures to pipeline orchestration owner.

playbooks.title: Stale source data
symptoms: Source-data product reports degraded, stale, partial, or unavailable supportability.
Market data, FX, benchmark, or readiness evidence is older than the requested as-of date.
dashboards_metrics: HTTP route-template status counts for integration routes

database_safe_checks.description: Read only latest source observation timestamps for the affected identifiers.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT security_id, max(price_date) AS latest_price_date FROM market_prices WHERE security_id = ANY(:security_ids) GROUP BY security_id;

containment_actions: Do not fabricate market, FX, benchmark, or external treasury evidence.
Keep degraded or unavailable posture visible to downstream services.
Route missing external data families to the owning bank-source integration backlog.
escalation_path: Escalate market/reference gaps to source-data operations.
Escalate external treasury or OMS gaps to the owning external integration team.

playbooks.title: Reconciliation failure
symptoms: Reconciliation run status is failed or completed with open findings.
Transaction-to-cashflow, position-valuation, or timeseries integrity checks report drift.
dashboards_metrics: HTTP route-template status counts for reconciliation routes

database_safe_checks.description: Read only reconciliation run and finding status for the affected run.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT run_id, status, business_date, completed_at FROM reconciliation_runs WHERE run_id = :run_id;

containment_actions: Keep affected portfolio supportability degraded until the finding is explained or remediated.
Do not mark breaks closed without source evidence.
Use reconciliation findings rather than free-text logs for follow-up.
escalation_path: Escalate data-quality breaks to the owning source-data family.
Escalate calculator consistency breaks to the owning calculator service.

playbooks.title: Readiness failure
symptoms: One or more services returns HTTP 503 from /health/ready.
Dependency statuses show timeout, unavailable, misconfigured, or error.

database_safe_checks.description: No database inspection is required until the readiness dependency map names database as degraded.

containment_actions: Do not route production traffic to services that fail readiness.
Use /version to confirm the running image before debugging a stale deployment.
Classify dependency failure before restarting services.
escalation_path: Escalate shared ingress or platform runtime issues to lotus-platform.
Escalate service-specific readiness code failures to the owning service.

playbooks.title: Database connectivity
symptoms: Readiness reports database timeout, unavailable, misconfigured, or error.
API routes fail with database connection errors or pool exhaustion.

database_safe_checks.description: Check Alembic state and a trivial read once connectivity is restored.

containment_actions: Do not run migrations or repair commands during active connectivity instability.
Preserve service logs and readiness responses before restarting.
Use app-local compose diagnostics only for local incidents.
escalation_path: Escalate managed database/network problems to platform operations.
Escalate schema drift to the migration owner.
post_incident_evidence: database logs or provider incident reference

playbooks.title: Kafka connectivity
symptoms: Consumers cannot poll, publish, commit, or route to DLQ.
Kafka topic creator fails or required topics are missing.

database_safe_checks.description: Read only ingestion/outbox backlog to determine blast radius after Kafka stabilizes.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT status, count(*) FROM ingestion_jobs GROUP BY status;

containment_actions: Pause replay and bulk ingestion while publish or commit failures are active.
Do not manually create topics outside the governed topic creator path.
Preserve DLQ and outbox evidence before any service restart.
escalation_path: Escalate broker, ACL, or topic-bootstrap failures to platform Kafka operations.
Escalate producer payload contract failures to the producing service owner.

playbooks.title: Security or audit denial spikes
symptoms: Enterprise authorization, read authorization, capability-rule, metrics-token, CORS, trusted-host, or payload-limit denials spike.
Audit write failures or missing audit evidence appear for protected operations.
dashboards_metrics: HTTP 401, 403, 413, and 400 counts by route template
enterprise authorization denial logs by bounded reason code

database_safe_checks.description: Use audit tables or log aggregation only; do not inspect protected payload values.
database_safe_checks.query: SELECT event_name, reason_code, count(*) FROM audit_events WHERE created_at >= :window_start GROUP BY event_name, reason_code;

containment_actions: Do not disable production security controls to clear denials.
Verify actor, role, capability, tenant, trusted host, CORS origin, and payload-size configuration.
Keep audit evidence source-safe; do not copy protected payload fields into incident notes.
escalation_path: Escalate IAM, ingress, WAF, and network policy issues to platform security operations.
Escalate service-local capability map drift to the owning service.
